chapter_title.en: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types

text: 2676 - وَ رَوَى اَلْبَزَنْطِيُّ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلْمَرْأَةِ تَضْرِبُ عَلَيْهَا اَلظِّلاَلَ وَ هِيَ مُحْرِمَةٌ فَقَالَ «نَعَمْ» قُلْتُ فَالرَّجُلُ يَضْرِبُ عَلَيْهِ اَلظِّلاَلَ وَ هُوَ مُحْرِمٌ قَالَ «نَعَمْ إِذَا كَانَتْ بِهِ شَقِيقَةٌ وَ يَتَصَدَّقُ بِمُدٍّ لِكُلِّ يَوْمٍ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2676 - Al-Bazanti narrated from Ali ibn Abi Hamzah, from Abu Basir, who said:
I asked Imam (as) about a woman placing a shade over herself while she is in Ihram.
Imam (as) said: "Yes."
I asked: "What about a man placing a shade over himself while in Ihram?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes, if he is afflicted with severe pain (shaqiqah), and he should give charity of a mudd for each day."
